description:
You are helping a junior DevOps engineer write and validate a **parallel backup utility**.  
All work must be done inside the normal user’s home: **/home/user** (no `sudo`).  
Follow the steps precisely; the automated grader will look for every file, directory and log entry exactly as described.

GENERAL REQUIREMENTS  
1. Create everything **inside the paths given below**.  
2. Write all shell code in POSIX-compatible **bash**.  
3. When a file must be executable, remember to grant `+x` permissions.  
4. At the end of the task, the utility **must already have been executed once** so that the first set of archives and the accompanying log file exist for inspection.

WORK BREAK-DOWN  

A) Prepare source data  
   1. Create two data directories  
      • /home/user/projects/data1  
      • /home/user/projects/data2  
   2. Populate them with exactly four small text files:  
      • /home/user/projects/data1/alpha.txt  →  contains the single line `alpha` followed by a UNIX newline (byte sequence: “alpha\n”).  
      • /home/user/projects/data1/beta.txt   →  contains the single line `beta\n`.  
      • /home/user/projects/data2/gamma.txt  →  contains the single line `gamma\n`.  
      • /home/user/projects/data2/delta.txt  →  contains the single line `delta\n`.

B) Implement the backup script  
   1. Path: **/home/user/bin/parallel_backup.sh** (create the /home/user/bin directory if it does not yet exist).  
   2. Hard-code the snapshot timestamp variable  
        SNAP_DATE="20230101T120000Z"  
      (This fixed value is essential for deterministic automated grading.)  
   3. The script must:  
      a. Ensure /home/user/archives exists (create if missing).  
      b. For every directory in the array DIRS=(/home/user/projects/data1 /home/user/projects/data2) run, **in parallel**,  
         `tar -czf /home/user/archives/<basename>_${SNAP_DATE}.tar.gz <directory>`  
         • Parallelism can be achieved with background jobs (`&`) **or** `xargs -P`.  
         • The script must call `wait` (or let `xargs` wait) so that it only proceeds once **all** archives are finished.  
      c. After archives are complete, gather for each archive:  
         • the byte size (`stat -c%s`)  
         • the lowercase hexadecimal SHA-256 checksum (`sha256sum | cut -d' ' -f1`)  
      d. Append one log line per archive to **/home/user/archives/backup_${SNAP_DATE}.log** using exactly this pipe-delimited format (one line per archive, sorted by the directory base-name alphabetically):  
  
         <SNAP_DATE>|<DIR_BASENAME>|<ARCHIVE_FILENAME>|<SIZE_BYTES>|<SHA256_DIGEST>
  
         Example of the required pattern (the numbers and digest will differ):  
         20230101T120000Z|data1|data1_20230101T120000Z.tar.gz|1234|ab12cd34…  
      e. The script itself must contain in-line comments briefly describing each major step.

C) Run the script once  
   • Execute /home/user/bin/parallel_backup.sh so that the first backup set is produced immediately while you work.

WHAT THE GRADER WILL CHECK  
1. Existence and exact contents of the four source files.  
2. Existence of /home/user/bin/parallel_backup.sh, its executable bit, and the presence of the hard-coded line `SNAP_DATE="20230101T120000Z"`.  
3. Presence of exactly two archives inside /home/user/archives/:  
      data1_20230101T120000Z.tar.gz  
      data2_20230101T120000Z.tar.gz  
4. Each archive must, when listed with `tar -tzf`, show only the files that belong to its respective source directory and nothing else.  
5. Existence of /home/user/archives/backup_20230101T120000Z.log with two lines that strictly follow the pipe-delimited schema. The first line must describe `data1`, the second `data2`.  
6. The SIZE_BYTES field must be a positive integer and must exactly match the actual file size on disk.  
7. The SHA256_DIGEST field must exactly match `sha256sum` of each corresponding archive.  
8. The script must use **parallel execution** (detected by the presence of `&` plus `wait`, or `xargs -P`, GNU `parallel`, etc.). Single-threaded solutions will be rejected.

LOG-FILE FORMAT SPECIFICATION (for automated parsing)  
• File: /home/user/archives/backup_20230101T120000Z.log  
• Each record is one line terminated by a single UNIX newline (`\n`).  
• Field order and separator: SNAP_DATE | DIR_BASENAME | ARCHIVE_FILENAME | SIZE_BYTES | SHA256_DIGEST  
• No extra spaces anywhere.  
• Lines must be sorted by DIR_BASENAME ascending (lexicographically).

IMPORTANT REMINDERS  
• Do not change the fixed timestamp value.  
• Do not compress multiple source directories into one archive—one archive per directory is mandatory.  
• Make sure every commanded path is spelled exactly as above (case-sensitive).  
• After completing the steps, double-check that re-running the script adds **additional** log lines (the grader will not test this but it is good practice).  
• The grader will only inspect paths under /home/user, so do not write anywhere else.
truth:
Initial state before agent starts:
1. The filesystem contains only default directories; none of the paths mentioned in the public spec exist yet.

State expected **after** successful completion:

Directories:
• /home/user/projects  
• /home/user/projects/data1  
• /home/user/projects/data2  
• /home/user/bin  
• /home/user/archives

Files and contents:

/home/user/projects/data1/alpha.txt
-----8<-----
alpha
-----8<-----

/home/user/projects/data1/beta.txt
-----8<-----
beta
-----8<-----

/home/user/projects/data2/gamma.txt
-----8<-----
gamma
-----8<-----

/home/user/projects/data2/delta.txt
-----8<-----
delta
-----8<-----

Script:
/home/user/bin/parallel_backup.sh
Key properties the test will look for:
• Executable bit set.
• Contains literal string: SNAP_DATE="20230101T120000Z"
• Contains either “&” followed later by “wait” OR the pattern “xargs -P”
• Produces archives into /home/user/archives
• Generates log lines as specified.

Archives that must exist:
/home/user/archives/data1_20230101T120000Z.tar.gz  
  (tar -tzf must list: projects/data1/alpha.txt and projects/data1/beta.txt OR data1/alpha.txt & data1/beta.txt; both patterns accepted)

/home/user/archives/data2_20230101T120000Z.tar.gz  
  (tar -tzf must list: projects/data2/gamma.txt and projects/data2/delta.txt OR data2/gamma.txt & data2/delta.txt)

/home/user/archives/backup_20230101T120000Z.log  
Two lines, exactly:
Line 1 starts with “20230101T120000Z|data1|data1_20230101T120000Z.tar.gz|”
Line 2 starts with “20230101T120000Z|data2|data2_20230101T120000Z.tar.gz|”
The SIZE_BYTES field must equal the output of `stat -c%s` for the respective archive and be numeric.
The SHA256_DIGEST field must equal `sha256sum` digest of the archive and be exactly 64 lowercase hex characters.

No extra files are allowed inside /home/user/archives except the two .tar.gz files and the single .log file.

The grader will recompute SHA-256 for each archive and compare it to the value present in the log; mismatch fails.

Parallelism verification:
The script file must match either regex:
  • `&[[:space:]]*#?.*wait`  (background jobs followed by wait)
  OR
  • `xargs[[:space:]].*-P[[:space:]]*[1-9]`
Absence of both patterns fails the test.
